subject: Public Health
title: The Eradication of Smallpox
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Eradication of Smallpox.

hook.text: In 1980, the WHO declared smallpox eradicated—the only human disease ever eliminated by human effort.

buildup.text: Smallpox killed 300 million people in the 20th century alone—with a 30% fatality rate.

discovery.text: Ring vaccination—vaccinating all contacts around each case—proved more effective than mass vaccination.

twist.text: The last natural case was Ali Maow Maalin in Somalia in 1977—a hospital cook who survived.

climax.text: Smallpox samples remain in two labs—the US and Russia—fueling debate about whether to destroy them.

punchline.text: Humanity's greatest victory was erasing a disease from the planet.

quiz.question: Where was the last natural case of smallpox?
